gaming_ai_thesis: BITKRAFT is the global early-stage investment platform exclusively focused on gaming, Web3, AI, and immersive media. Founded by esports pioneer Jens Hilgers (co-founder of ESL and G2 Esports), BITKRAFT operates six specialized funds and has backed 135+ companies across six continents. They invest heavily in AI-powered gaming infrastructure, AI-generated content for games, and synthetic reality technologies. Recent investments include AI-native gaming platforms, procedural content generation tools, and dynamic world-building technologies.
geographic_focus: Global (Berlin, LA, SF, NYC, London, Singapore)
outreach_angle: BITKRAFT's newly raised $275M Fund 3 (June 2025) is actively deploying in AI-gaming intersections. Their 'Agents of Change' thesis on AI in gaming explicitly targets autonomous agents and procedural world generation. Carlos leads crypto and gaming investments at the intersection of AI and interactive media.

gaming_ai_thesis: AI Grant is a unique accelerator program for AI-native companies founded by Daniel Gross (former Y Combinator Head of AI, Pioneer founder) and Nat Friedman (former GitHub CEO). They provide $250K grants plus $250K-$600K in Azure/compute credits on maximally founder-friendly terms (no-cap, no-discount MFN SAFE). Beyond capital, they operate the Andromeda Cluster—a supercomputer with 4,000+ H100 GPUs exclusively for portfolio companies. Their thesis centers on fundamental AI research applied to real-world problems, including AI agents, generative content, and synthetic environments.
outreach_angle: AI Grant's unique compute advantage via the Andromeda Cluster (4,000+ H100s) provides immediate infrastructure for training AI gaming models. Nat's GitHub background means appreciation for developer tools and infrastructure plays. The grant-first approach de-risks early-stage AI gaming experiments.

gaming_ai_thesis: Lux Capital invests in emerging science and technology ventures that aim to create a fantastic future through feats of engineering. Founded in 2000, they focus on deep tech including AI, robotics, spatiotemporal computing, and next-generation computing platforms. Shahin Farshchi leads investments in AI, robotics, and computing infrastructure with a focus on 'Riskgaming'—using game theory and simulation for strategic decision-making. Their AI thesis includes foundation models, AI infrastructure, and applications of AI to complex systems including gaming and simulation.
outreach_angle: Lux's 'Riskgaming' initiative and Q4 2024 report on Moravec's Paradox show deep engagement with AI gaming/simulation intersections. Shahin's focus on AI infrastructure and computing platforms aligns with AI-native gaming engines. The fresh $1.5B Fund IX provides ample capital for seed-stage AI infrastructure bets.

gaming_ai_thesis: Polychain Capital is the premier cryptocurrency investment fund, founded in 2016 by Olaf Carlson-Wee (first employee at Coinbase). They invest in protocols and companies advancing blockchain technology and the global adoption of cryptocurrencies. Their gaming thesis centers on blockchain-native games, on-chain game mechanics, AI-crypto convergence for autonomous agents, and digital property rights in virtual worlds. They have a strong track record in Web3 gaming infrastructure and game-theoretic systems.
notable_gaming_investments: Dapper Labs (NBA Top Shot, Flow blockchain)
outreach_angle: Olaf founded Polychain after being Coinbase's first employee—he understands both crypto infrastructure and platform scaling. Polychain's investment in Dapper Labs and Immutable X shows commitment to gaming infrastructure. Their thesis on 'game-theoretic equilibrium' in blockchain systems directly applies to AI-powered game economies.

gaming_ai_thesis: Maven 11 is a crypto-native venture capital firm founded in 2015 in Amsterdam. They partner with 'brave outliers challenging the status quo by redefining ownership' through blockchain and Web3 technologies. Their 2024 predictions explicitly highlight the convergence of crypto and AI in gaming, with portfolio companies building infrastructure for next-generation gaming experiences. They launched a Founder Residency Program in 2025 for earliest-stage support and actively invest in the AI-crypto intersection.
outreach_angle: Maven 11's freshly raised $107M Fund III (March 2025) is explicitly focused on the AI-crypto intersection and gaming infrastructure. Their 2024 predictions call out the emergence of gaming-specific blockchain infrastructure. Balder's team works 'in the trenches' with founders—a hands-on approach that fits NEXUS AI's technical complexity.
